game_name: Might and Magic II: Gates to Another World

danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based enemy graphics in combat screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring battle engagement; variety indicates escalating danger levels
High-danger zones containing boss encounters and puzzles; spatial complexity indicates hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: Four distinct elemental-themed dungeons with altered color palettes
danger_signals.meaning: Environment-specific hazards; progression into dangerous late-game content

danger_signals.visual_form: Character status display showing age counter
danger_signals.meaning: Persistent threat mechanic; approaching old age (~75) triggers random death risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Final dungeon spaceship environment, distinct sci-fi aesthetic departure
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate threat manifestation; visual break from fantasy signals end-game danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Tavern/inn building sprites marking rest points
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for character storage, healing, and party restructuring
Non-combat zones for trading, quests, and information gathering

safety_signals.visual_form: Cartographer skill unlocks map display overlay
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation safety; reduces disorientation in dungeon exploration
Safe hub for character stat improvement via Cupie doll collection
Curative safe zone; reverses character aging damage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Associated with Corak NPC and his soul/body narrative
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Isle of the Ancients contains dual wizard manifestations (good/evil)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sheltem entity from original game; plague-like encroachment theme
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Final spaceship dungeon enemies; spread across four elemental planes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Elemental planes; final sci-fi spaceship; temporal anomalies

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Barbarian enemies restricted to Barbarian class areas

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Locations tied to Ninja class quest objectives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map shift from Varn to CRON (Central Research Observational Nacelle) with new tileset palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party imports from first game; narrative continuation through character progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Corrupted/altered environment visuals in multiple regions; spread across four elemental planes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Quest urgency communicated through NPC dialogue; multiple branching objectives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Time travel mechanic accessing nine different centuries; altered visual states of same locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Only two centuries mechanically relevant to main story; quest-specific temporal requirements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Status screen age counter; visual indicator approaching death threshold (~75)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Random death triggers at old age; failing age-reversal spells increase age instead; resort spa offers curative service

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Class-restricted dungeon areas with distinct visual styling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party forced to split or recombine; hiring hirelings fills gaps; class-specific quest environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spaceship dungeon with sci-fi aesthetic (stark departure from fantasy); encrypted message puzzle environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time-limited encrypted message solving required to escape planet; thematic escalation to space travel

environmental_storytelling.example: Scattered nonsensical colored messages across land combine into useful hints; requires exploration and pattern recognition
Areas restricted to specific classes force narrative splits; communicate faction/skill barriers through spatial design
Four elemental planes with distinct visual palettes communicate Sheltem's dimensional corruption spreading
Taverns with humorous food selections; circus grounds with Cupie doll mechanics; health spa communicating restorative purpose
Sex-change device in cave; absurdist elements like alcohol intoxication system; communicate world's magical chaos
Twenty-six character creation slots stored at inns; hireling payment systems signal economic world-state
Same locations rendered differently across nine centuries; communicate time travel mechanic's world impact

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Active in party (max 6 initial, up to 8 with hirelings)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Level progression (up to 255); hit points scale to 65535; magic points to 9999; stat growth at circus; age increment each game day
Remain at designated inn location; accessible for swapping; serve as character persistence mechanic
Controlled identically to player characters; require daily gold payment; cost scales with level; provide party flexibility
Random death triggers; age-reversal spells fail and increase age; spa treatment required to reset aging
Area visually blocked; forces party split or character swapping; enforces quest pacing
Temporary stat reduction; communication of consumable consequence mechanic
Up to 255 monsters per battle; hit point range 1-64000; escalating difficulty through numeric scope and encounter size
Encrypted message puzzle with time limit; thematic shift to sci-fi; communicates ultimate Sheltem threat context

notes: Might and Magic II communicates world-state primarily through environmental visual shifts (elemental planes, temporal locations), character status mechanics (aging, level caps, skill display), and class-gated spatial design. Danger escalation moves from dungeon crawling to dimensional corruption to sci-fi spaceship finale. Safety zones are explicitly marked through building sprites and NPC presence. Political/faction information is conveyed through quest NPCs (Corak, Sheltem, class antagonists) and class-specific restricted areas. The game's unusual numeric scope (extreme level/HP caps) and absurdist elements (sex change device, intoxication) communicate a world of magical chaos and experimental game systems. Progression signals shift from fantasy to sci-fi, signaling narrative escalation toward final confrontation.
